purpose: Machine-readable Display Pack v2 descriptor boundary for MAS medical display packs, paper-facing quality refs, and the OPL Pack OS handoff tail.
machine_boundary: This contract declares the required descriptor fields and authority boundaries for MAS Display Pack v2. The validator lives in med_autoscience.display_pack_v2_contract. It does not claim that MAS owns a generic OPL Pack OS.

field_roles.version: Exact semantic version for reproducible paper locks.
field_roles.source: Where the pack descriptor and assets resolve from.
field_roles.owner: Pack maintainer or responsible domain owner.
field_roles.templates: Template descriptors exposed by this pack.
field_roles.style_profiles: Pack-local style profile refs for publication displays.
field_roles.ai_policy: AI illustration and AI visual-review policy refs.
field_roles.exemplars: Link-only exemplar refs and intake provenance.
field_roles.provenance: Source, lock, hash, release, and audit provenance refs.
field_roles.opl_handoff: Refs-only handoff to the external OPL-owned generic Pack OS.

template_descriptor.authority_boundary: Template descriptors bind renderer, input schema, QC, style, golden, and exemplar refs. They do not authorize publication readiness or replace paper-level quality receipts.

quality_surfaces.boundary: display_pack_lock.json and submission manifests preserve refs, status, and hashes. They do not become publication verdicts or artifact authority.

external_opl_consumer.boundary: OPL can consume this MAS Display Pack v2 contract and emit a generic pack lock/audit smoke receipt. This consumer preserves MAS authority boundaries and does not land publication authority inside MAS.

opl_handoff.handoff_rule: MAS exposes refs and boundaries for OPL-owned Pack OS consumers. OPL repo has landed generic Pack OS install, registry, cache, distribution, lock, validation, and MAS Display Pack smoke surfaces; MAS still does not own or close generic Pack OS inside this repository.
forbidden_claims: MAS owns generic OPL Pack OS
Display Pack v2 contract closes generic Pack OS inside MAS
OPL Pack OS can write MAS publication truth
OPL Pack OS can authorize MAS publication readiness

external_opl_pack_os_substrate.boundary: Generic install, registry, cache, distribute, lock, validate, and MAS display smoke surfaces are landed in the OPL repo. MAS only references these external OPL-owned surfaces and still does not own generic Pack OS or publication authority.
